description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a predominantly gray coloration and a speckled texture. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring small inclusions of darker minerals and occasional lighter patches, giving it a mottled appearance. One side of the stone displays a prominent protrusion with a cluster of embedded mineral fragments, some of which have a reddish hue. The stone's form is generally rounded but with several angular edges and visible cracks, suggesting natural weathering. Its overall mass and density indicate it is composed mainly of granite or a similar igneous rock. The stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rough, gray, speckled stone with irregular shape and mineral inclusions, featuring a prominent protrusion on one side. Its surface is weathered and uneven, typical of natural landscaping stones.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's most prominent protrusion with visible mineral inclusions and cracks.
description_view_1: This side shows a more uniform, rounded surface with fewer inclusions and a smoother appearance.
description_view_2: This angle reveals a relatively flat face with subtle speckling and minimal protrusions.
description_view_3: This view displays the stone's broadest, most continuous surface, with fine speckling and slight surface undulations.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled speckling with scattered mineral inclusions and occasional cracks; no regular or artificial patterning.
attribute_weathering: Rough and cracked with visible signs of natural weathering, but no significant erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping stones; may contrast with manicured lawns or paved surfaces.
